skill.name: lab-inventory-predictor
skill.description: Predict depletion time of critical lab reagents based on historical usage frequency, and automatically generate purchase alerts when stock falls below safety thresholds.
skill.summary: Predict reagent depletion and auto-generate purchase alerts from lab usage history.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 70 static analysis findings across 4 files (1079 lines). All 70 findings are false positives: the 19 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections matched argparse calls and markdown formatting tables, not real crypto code. The 40 'shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code blocks documenting CLI usage, not executable commands. The 9 filesystem findings relate to legitimate local JSON data persistence and documented path traversal protections. The 2 network findings are GitHub source URLs in documentation. No prompt injection attempts were detected. The skill is a legitimate local-only lab inventory management tool with no security concerns.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines in SKILL.md, scripts/main.py, and audit_result_v4.json as using weak cryptographic algorithms. The actual code contains no cryptographic operations. The audit JSON file is a pre-computed artifact, not executable source. Line 380 in main.py is argparse, not crypto. SKILL.md matches are markdown table formatting.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution in Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 40 instances of backtick patterns in SKILL.md. These are all markdown code blocks showing example CLI commands and Python API usage in documentation. No commands are executed dynamically.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access for Data Storage (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Line 73 uses os.path.expanduser to create a default data path in the home directory (~/.openclaw/workspace/data/lab-inventory.json). This is standard practice for application config/data storage and does not access system files or hidden user data.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged '..' patterns in SKILL.md. The documentation describes path traversal protection that rejects paths containing '../', not actual traversal execution. The code implements a security guard.

content.user_title: Predict Reagent Depletion and Automate Purchase Alerts
content.value_statement: Laboratory researchers often run out of critical reagents unexpectedly, leading to costly experiment delays. This skill analyzes historical usage data to predict depletion dates and sends automated alerts before stock runs out.
actual_capabilities: Predicts reagent depletion dates based on historical usage frequency
Generates purchase alerts when stock falls below safety thresholds
Tracks current stock levels and usage history for multiple reagents
Produces inventory reports in text, JSON, or CSV format
Flags low-confidence predictions when fewer than 3 usage records exist
Calculates daily consumption rates with configurable lookback periods
limitations: Requires historical usage data for accurate predictions
Cannot predict depletion for reagents with fewer than 2 usage records
Does not connect to external enterprise inventory management systems
Assumes linear consumption patterns for depletion calculations

use_cases.title: Monitor Lab Reagent Inventory
use_cases.description: Track current stock levels for all lab reagents and view their depletion status at a glance.

use_cases.title: Plan Experiment Consumables
use_cases.description: Predict when specific reagents will run out during long experiment series and order replacements in time.

use_cases.title: Record Daily Reagent Usage
use_cases.description: Log reagent consumption after each experiment to maintain accurate stock records and improve predictions.

prompt_templates.title: Check Reagent Inventory Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the current status of all my lab reagents.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of all reagent stock levels and depletion dates

prompt_templates.title: Add and Track a New Reagent
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a new reagent called PBS Buffer with 500ml current stock, 7 safety days, and 3 days lead time. Then record a usage of 50ml for the Cell Culture experiment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a new reagent and logging the first usage record

prompt_templates.title: Generate Purchase Alerts Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete inventory report showing all reagents that need to be ordered soon.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing all active alerts before placing a supply order

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Depletion with Confidence Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Predict depletion for PBS Buffer and tell me how confident the prediction is based on the number of usage records available.
prompt_templates.scenario: Validating prediction reliability before making purchase decisions

output_examples.input: Show me the current status of all my lab reagents.
output_examples.output:
Inventory Status (3 reagents total)

Reagent Name        Current Stock    Daily Consumption    Predicted Depletion    Status
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PBS Buffer          450.00 ml        12.50 ml/day         2026-06-15              🟢 normal
DMEM Media          200.00 ml        25.00 ml/day         2026-06-05              🟡 warning
Trypsin             50.00 ml         8.00 ml/day          2026-05-31              🔴 critical

output_examples.output:
Purchase Reminders (2 total)

🔴 [CRITICAL] Trypsin
   Current stock: 50.00 ml
   Reason: Estimated depletion in 3 days; Past purchase deadline (3 day lead time)
   Predicted depletion: 2026-05-31

🟡 [WARNING] DMEM Media
   Current stock: 200.00 ml
   Reason: Estimated depletion in 8 days
   Predicted depletion: 2026-06-05

best_practices: Record reagent usage after every experiment so predictions stay accurate over time.
Set safety days to match your supplier delivery time plus a buffer for unexpected delays.
Review purchase alerts regularly and place orders before depletion dates arrive.
anti_patterns: Using the predictor without recording any usage history, which prevents all depletion calculations.
Setting safety thresholds too low, causing alerts to trigger only after stock is already critical.
Ignoring low-confidence warnings for reagents with fewer than 3 usage records.

faq.question: What data does this skill need to work?
faq.answer: The skill needs reagent names, current stock quantities, and historical usage records with amounts and dates.

faq.question: How are depletion dates calculated?
faq.answer: Depletion is calculated by dividing current stock by the daily consumption rate, which is derived from average usage over the lookback period.

faq.question: What happens if I have insufficient usage data?
faq.answer: When fewer than 2 usage records exist, the skill cannot calculate a consumption rate and returns insufficient data instead of guessing.

faq.question: How are purchase alerts triggered?
faq.answer: Alerts trigger when days until depletion is less than or equal to safety days plus lead time, or when stock falls below the safety stock threshold.

faq.question: Can I use this with any type of lab material?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill works with any measurable lab material tracked by quantity and usage, including buffers, media, reagents, and consumables.

faq.question: Can I undo an accidental usage recording?
faq.answer: You can update or remove a reagent and re-record its correct stock level, but individual usage records cannot be deleted after they are added.
